steps.title: Evaluate both sides at x = 1 to create a constraint
steps.content:
Since the original expression must equal $$\frac{a}{4}x^{2}+\frac{b}{4}x+\frac{c}{4}$$ for all values of $x$, choose $x=1$ because it turns the right side into $\frac{a+b+c}{4}$. At $x=1$:
$$-16(5(1)-3)^2+4(5(1)-2)^2=-16(2)^2+4(3)^2=-64+36=-28$$
So $$\frac{a+b+c}{4}=-28$$

steps.title: Solve for a + b + c
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $4$:
$$a+b+c=4(-28)=-112$$
This matches, so A is correct.
